title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is methodological: several fresh papers probe whether current evaluation, alignment, and control techniques are actually measuring or enforcing what they claim. The mix spans jailbreak behavior, evaluator bias, agent verification, security benchmarking, privacy leakage, and multimodal bias, pointing to a continued shift from headline capabilities toward auditing reliability and failure modes.

items.title: Efficient and Sound Probabilistic Verification for AI Agents
items.why_it_matters: Extends formal runtime verification toward uncertain agent behavior, improving enforceable safety guarantees in real deployments.

items.title: How Fragile Are Training-Free AI-Generated Image Detectors? A Controlled Audit of Score Direction, Preprocessing, and Compression
items.why_it_matters: Shows detector performance can hinge on brittle evaluation choices, weakening confidence in synthetic-media safeguards.

items.title: Calibration Without Comprehension: Diagnosing the Limits of Fine-Tuning LLMs for Vulnerability Detection in Systems Software
items.why_it_matters: Suggests strong benchmark scores may overstate real security reasoning, complicating safe use in vulnerability workflows.

items.title: What Do Safety-Aligned LLMs Learn From Mixed Compliance Demonstrations?
items.why_it_matters: Examines how in-context examples can shift refusal behavior, informing jailbreak resilience and safety training design.

items.title: Contagion Networks: Evaluator Bias Propagation in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
items.why_it_matters: Highlights how biased model judges can systematically distort multi-agent outputs and downstream oversight.

items.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
items.why_it_matters: Identifies concrete visual features behind multimodal bias, aiding targeted audits and mitigation strategies.

items.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
items.why_it_matters: Tests whether latent-space reasoning reduces interpretability, a key issue for debugging and assurance.

items.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
items.why_it_matters: Proposes separating agent reasoning from execution authority to reduce high-impact autonomous misuse.

items.title: A-COMPASS: Formal Foundations for Anonymity Analysis in Microdata
items.why_it_matters: Strengthens formal privacy analysis for released datasets, relevant to governance and compliance.

items.title: From Efficiency to Leakage -- Privacy Backdoor in Federated Language Model Fine-Tuning
items.why_it_matters: Flags a privacy risk in parameter-efficient federated tuning, challenging assumptions about data protection.

items.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
items.why_it_matters: Explores scalable alignment signals but raises immediate consent and user-privacy concerns.

items.title: Improving health intelligence in ChatGPT
items.why_it_matters: Physician-informed evaluations for health responses show a deployment-specific safety and quality assurance approach.

items.title: When Companies Listen to Employees About AI, Everyone Benefits
items.why_it_matters: Reinforces internal governance as a practical mechanism for surfacing deployment risks early.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
